title: Cyclosorus bordenii
intro: Cyclosorus bordenii, also known as the Borden's maiden fern, is a type of fern species belonging to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus bordenii include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, this plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in the form of a tea, or as a tincture, to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus bordenii is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics of the plant. Cyclosorus bordenii is a rare species native to certain regions of the world.
